args.content:
Hawaii Packing List
===================
Based on Vacation Plans: June 10th – June 20th, 2024

CLOTHING
--------
- Swimwear (2–3 suits)
- Lightweight casual outfits (shorts, t-shirts, sundresses)
- One semi-formal outfit for the Luau (Polynesian Cultural Center, June 16)
- Light jacket or hoodie for cooler evenings
- Undergarments and socks
- Comfortable walking shoes
- Hiking boots or trail shoes (for Diamond Head, June 13)
- Flip-flops / sandals
- Hat or cap for sun protection
- Sunglasses

BEACH & WATER GEAR
-------------------
- Reef-safe sunscreen (SPF 30+)
- After-sun lotion / aloe vera gel
- Snorkeling gear (mask, snorkel) – if not renting at Hanauma Bay (June 12)
- Water shoes (for rocky areas at Hanauma Bay and Kailua Beach)
- Quick-dry towel
- Waterproof dry bag
- Swim goggles
- Rash guard / UV-protective shirt (for kayaking, June 18)

HIKING & OUTDOOR
-----------------
- Daypack / small backpack (for Diamond Head, June 13, and North Shore road trip, June 15)
- Reusable water bottle
- Insect repellent
- Trail snacks / energy bars
- Light rain jacket (in case of tropical showers)
- First-aid kit (band-aids, antiseptic wipes, blister pads)

TRAVEL ESSENTIALS
------------------
- Passport / ID
- Flight tickets / boarding passes
- Hotel reservation confirmations
- Travel insurance documents
- Credit/debit cards and some cash
- Printed itinerary and maps

ELECTRONICS
-----------
- Smartphone and charger
- Camera / GoPro (with extra batteries and memory cards)
- Portable power bank
- Waterproof phone pouch
- Universal power adapter (if needed)

PERSONAL CARE & HEALTH
-----------------------
- Toothbrush, toothpaste, floss
- Shampoo, conditioner, body wash
- Deodorant
- Hairbrush / comb
- Razor / shaving supplies
- Prescription medications
- Motion sickness tablets (for boat/road trips)

MISCELLANEOUS
--------------
- Beach bag or tote
- Book or e-reader (for relaxation day, June 17)
- Snorkeling confirmation voucher (Hanauma Bay, June 12)
- Luau tickets (June 16)
- Reusable shopping bag (Ala Moana Center, June 19)
- Ziplock bags (to protect electronics from sand/water)

NOTES
-----
- Check weather forecast a few days before departure and adjust accordingly.
- Most snorkeling and kayaking rental shops provide gear, but bringing your own ensures comfort.
- Pearl Harbor (June 14) has a no-bag policy; plan accordingly with only essentials.
